title: Paspalum brachystachyum
taxonomy: Paspalum brachystachyum is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is a type of tropical grass native to the Americas. Within the family Poaceae, Paspalum brachystachyum is further classified as belonging to the genus Paspalum. The genus Paspalum is a large and diverse group of grasses that are often found in a variety of environments. Paspalum brachystachyum is also sometimes referred to as a type of panicoideae, a subfamily of grasses that are characterized by their panicled inflorescences.

intro: Paspalum brachystachyum, also known as Puna grass or Paspalum, is a tropical grass native to South America. The plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it a potential natural remedy for various ailments. The bioactive constituents of Paspalum brachystachyum include flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Puna grass is commonly used in traditional herbal medicine and is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, as well as being used in the form of capsules or tinctures to harness its health benefits.
